active.description: Active state. If false the session is no longer active.

authenticated_at.description:
The Session Authentication Timestamp

When this session was authenticated at. If multi-factor authentication was used this
is the time when the last factor was authenticated (e.g. the TOTP code challenge was completed).

authentication_methods.description: A list of authenticators which were used to authenticate the session.

items.description: A singular authenticator used during authentication / login.

aal.description:
The authenticator assurance level can be one of "aal1", "aal2", or "aal3". A higher number means that it is harder
for an attacker to compromise the account.

Generally, "aal1" implies that one authentication factor was used while AAL2 implies that two factors (e.g.
password + TOTP) have been used.

To learn more about these levels please head over to: https://www.ory.com/kratos/docs/concepts/credentials
aal.title: Authenticator Assurance Level (AAL)

completed_at.description: When the authentication challenge was completed.

method.description:
The method used in this authenticator.
password CredentialsTypePassword
oidc CredentialsTypeOIDC
totp CredentialsTypeTOTP
lookup_secret CredentialsTypeLookup
webauthn CredentialsTypeWebAuthn
code CredentialsTypeCodeAuth
passkey CredentialsTypePasskey
profile CredentialsTypeProfile
saml CredentialsTypeSAML
deviceauthn CredentialsTypeDeviceAuthn
identifier_first CredentialsTypeIdentifierFirst
link_recovery CredentialsTypeRecoveryLink  CredentialsTypeRecoveryLink is a special credential type linked to the link strategy (recovery flow).  It is not used within the credentials object itself.
code_recovery CredentialsTypeRecoveryCode

link_recovery CredentialsTypeRecoveryLink  CredentialsTypeRecoveryLink is a special credential type linked to the link strategy (recovery flow).  It is not used within the credentials object itself.
code_recovery CredentialsTypeRecoveryCode

organization.description: The Organization id used for authentication

provider.description: OIDC or SAML provider id used for authentication

upstream_acr.description:
UpstreamACR is the `acr` claim reported by the upstream OIDC
provider, if any. Populated only for OIDC login methods when the
upstream ID token contained an `acr` claim.

upstream_amr.description:
UpstreamAMR is the `amr` claim reported by the upstream OIDC
provider, if any. Populated only for OIDC login methods when the
upstream ID token contained an `amr` claim.

items.title: AuthenticationMethod identifies an authentication method

authentication_methods.title: List of (Used) AuthenticationMethods

authenticator_assurance_level.description:
The authenticator assurance level can be one of "aal1", "aal2", or "aal3". A higher number means that it is harder
for an attacker to compromise the account.

Generally, "aal1" implies that one authentication factor was used while AAL2 implies that two factors (e.g.
password + TOTP) have been used.

To learn more about these levels please head over to: https://www.ory.com/kratos/docs/concepts/credentials
authenticator_assurance_level.title: Authenticator Assurance Level (AAL)

devices.description: Devices has history of all endpoints where the session was used

location.description: Geo Location corresponding to the IP Address

expires_at.description:
The Session Expiry

When this session expires at.

identity.description: An [identity](https://www.ory.com/docs/kratos/concepts/identity-user-model) represents a (human) user in Ory.

created_at.description: CreatedAt is a helper struct field for gobuffalo.pop.

additionalProperties.description: Credentials represents a specific credential type

config.title: JSONRawMessage represents a json.RawMessage that works well with JSON, SQL, and Swagger.

created_at.description: CreatedAt is a helper struct field for gobuffalo.pop.

identifiers.description: Identifiers represent a list of unique identifiers this credential type matches.

Type discriminates between different types of credentials.
password CredentialsTypePassword
oidc CredentialsTypeOIDC
totp CredentialsTypeTOTP

code CredentialsTypeCodeAuth
passkey CredentialsTypePasskey

identifier_first CredentialsTypeIdentifierFirst
link_recovery CredentialsTypeRecoveryLink  CredentialsTypeRecoveryLink is a special credential type linked to the link strategy (recovery flow).  It is not used within the credentials object itself.

updated_at.description: UpdatedAt is a helper struct field for gobuffalo.pop.

version.description: Version refers to the version of the credential. Useful when changing the config schema.

additionalProperties.title: identityCredentials

credentials.description: Credentials represents all credentials that can be used for authenticating this identity.

external_id.description:
ExternalID is an optional external ID of the identity. This is used to link
the identity to an external system. If set, the external ID must be unique
across all identities.

id.description:
ID is the identity's unique identifier.

The Identity ID can not be changed and can not be chosen. This ensures future
compatibility and optimization for distributed stores such as CockroachDB.

metadata_admin.description: NullJSONRawMessage represents a json.RawMessage that works well with JSON, SQL, and Swagger and is NULLable-
metadata_admin.title: nullJsonRawMessage

metadata_public.description: NullJSONRawMessage represents a json.RawMessage that works well with JSON, SQL, and Swagger and is NULLable-
metadata_public.title: nullJsonRawMessage

recovery_addresses.description: RecoveryAddresses contains all the addresses that can be used to recover an identity.

created_at.description: CreatedAt is a helper struct field for gobuffalo.pop.

updated_at.description: UpdatedAt is a helper struct field for gobuffalo.pop.

region.description:
Region is the Ory Network region this identity is homed in. Set by
the multi-region persister; empty on OSS and single-region deployments.
eu-central EUCentral
asia-northeast AsiaNorthEast
us-east USEast
us-west USWest
eu EU
asia Asia
us US
global Global

us-east USEast
us-west USWest

us US
global Global

schema_id.description: SchemaID is the ID of the JSON Schema to be used for validating the identity's traits.

schema_url.description:
SchemaURL is the URL of the endpoint where the identity's traits schema can be fetched from.

format: url

state.description:
State is the identity's state.

This value has currently no effect.
active StateActive
inactive StateInactive

traits.description:
Traits represent an identity's traits. The identity is able to create, modify, and delete traits
in a self-service manner. The input will always be validated against the JSON Schema defined
in `schema_url`.
traits.title: identityTraits

updated_at.description: UpdatedAt is a helper struct field for gobuffalo.pop.

verifiable_addresses.description: VerifiableAddresses contains all the addresses that can be verified by the user.

items.description: VerifiableAddress is an identity's verifiable address

status.description: VerifiableAddressStatus must not exceed 16 characters as that is the limitation in the SQL Schema
status.title: identityVerifiableAddressStatus

updated_at.description: When this entry was last updated

verified.description: Indicates if the address has already been verified

items.title: verifiableIdentityAddress

identity.title: Identity represents an Ory Kratos identity

issued_at.description:
The Session Issuance Timestamp

When this session was issued at. Usually equal or close to `authenticated_at`.

tokenized.description:
Tokenized is the tokenized (e.g. JWT) version of the session.

It is only set when the `tokenize_as` query parameter was set to a valid tokenize template during calls to `/session/whoami`.

schema.description: The standard Ory JSON API error format.

debug.description: Debug contains debug information. This is usually not available and has to be enabled.
debug.example: The database adapter was unable to find the element

error.example: The requested resource could not be found

error_description.description: Description contains further information on the nature of the error.
error_description.example: Object with ID 12345 does not exist

id.description:
ID is a unique error ID.
feature_not_available ErrFeatureNotAvailable
quota_exceeded ErrQuotaExceeded

status_code.description: Code represents the error status code (404, 403, 401, ...).

schema.title: JSON API Error Response
